metadata.description: A human identity/account — an ACTOR that DCM authenticates and authorizes (ADR-RBAC-001). Fills the named-but-unbuilt Identity.* slot (naming-conventions §2; resource-type-hierarchy §Identity). Holds NO secret material — credentials are referenced (Security.CredentialRef). Backs the built-in auth provider's local users and, when federated, references the external IdP subject (PIP). Its actor_type is `human`, from the one closed actor-type vocabulary (universal-audit).

handle.description: Login handle / username, unique within the realization.

actor_type.description: From the closed actor-type vocabulary (universal-audit); a Person is always `human`.

authenticated_by.description: handle/uuid of the Auth Provider (auth capability) or Security.DirectoryService that authenticates this person. Default: the built-in provider (no external IdP required).

credential_ref.description: Reference to a Security.CredentialRef — never inline secret material (CPX-001).

external_subject.description: The external IdP subject id when federated (a PIP reference, resolved not stored).

actor_uuid.description: The stable actor id referenced by role-assignments and by DCMGroup (access_grouping) membership.

relationships.description: Member of an access DCMGroup (access_grouping) or Identity.Group; membership drives role binding (ADR-RBAC-001).
